turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: I think you have a moment or so, Mr. Ringold.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice and may it please the Court. Mr. Justice Whittaker, in response to a question concerning the necessity or the right to a hearing, it was our opinion that this statute in this particular situation deprived the plaintiffs of a hearing. For that reason, at its inception, our complaint was drawn on that basis and paragraph 5 of our complaint as it appears on page 2 of the printed record, upon they did execute such oath, his employment be terminated. Thereupon, there was a denial by the State. Request for admissions were made which admitted that the statute would be construed in that fashion to deny a hearing. The findings of fact in conclusions of law, conclusion, Finding of Fact number 6 was then included.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: You say there was a stipulation in the trial below that -- to the effect that that he would not be -- these people would not be accorded a hearing.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Request for admission were made and in -- and the findings of fact were based upon the answers to the requests for admissions.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Well, I thought you said --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: -- from the matter of stipulation just a moment ago.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I said their answers, Mr. Chief Justice --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: -- to our request for admissions, indicated that a hearing would not be granted.

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: Do you remember what the number of that, Mr. Ringold?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I do not believe that is in the printed record.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: But it --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I would have to go through --

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: Number 5?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Two or five. In any event --

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: In any event --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Let me ask you this, Mr. Ringold. In your brief, you state that -- that the 1955 law repealed that portion of the law which -- which accorded a hearing.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: This --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Now, and Mr. Fuller says that that law as it set out on -- on page 67, 68 of his brief is still a law and that such a hearing can be held. Will you tell us why there is that difference of opinion between you and what you base it on?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: The question I assume, Mr. Chief Justice, depended upon whether or not a review by implication took place or not. This was why we interpreted as a review by implication having taken place by virtue of the 1955 Act. And again, that was why we framed the complaint originally to specifically raise the question. And this matter was argued in the lower court, it was argued in the Supreme Court of the State of Washington. We of course extensively argued it with respect to the Attorney General's list. We did raise the question with respect to a -- we -- we cited Slochower and those other matters. Then when the original opinion of the Supreme Court came down, the focal point then, it became much more pointed as to whether or not the hearing issue was raised. And in our petition for rehearing, pages 41 to 45 of the unprinted record, we specifically raise the --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: What did you say? And could you just read us a sentence or two to see that --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor. The Wyman case clearly establish that public employment was not a privilege which could be denied for any reason whatsoever. The United States Supreme Court's unanimous holding was that eligibility for public employment can only be conditioned on requirements which had a reasonable relation to fitness. The importance of this aspect of our argument was totally overlooked by this Court in its failure to mention and apply the doctrine of Swayze, Schwer, Canniburgh, Slochower. It is --

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I am reading from the unprinted --

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: This is our petition for rehearing.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: But you can leave that with us, can't you? Or else I should --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: It is in the record.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: -- the other side would agree to this. (Inaudible)if they agreed to this.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes, it is in the record.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Do we have that --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: -- for rehearing?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes, Mr. Justice Black. It is no answer to say Learner Dylan and Adler. Each of these cases involved hearings to determine the fitness of the employee for employment under the state's right to investigate the qualifications of the employees. And then we underlined. "There is not one case decided by the Supreme Court of the United States which holds that the State may discharge an employee solely upon refusal to subscribe to an oath that he is not a subversive person."

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Am I -- am I wrong, as I read complete through your 86-paged brief in the Supreme Court too hurriedly to have missed that you discussed this opinion in that brief.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Page 35 of our brief, Your Honor.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Under what heading does that come? And just with the -- the heading, thinking that the heading --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Under due process.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: The first point was the hearing as to the nature of the organization, is that right?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Court please, page 35 of the respondent's brief in the Supreme Court of the State of Washington. In Slochower versus Board or Education's --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: What is the heading under which this appears?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: The heading under which it appeared is under due process that scienter is a necessary element in oaths of nonmembership in proscribed organizations. We then cited Slochower. The plaintiff claimed this privilege, we quoted Mr. Justice Clark, then we said the Court discusses Adler Garner and then says, “But in each of these cases, it was emphasized that the State must conform to the requirements of due process. The summary dismissal of appellant violates due process of law.” This is emphasized, Mr. Justice Frankfurter.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Do you think that's the discussion of the requirement of a hearing?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I -- well, we certainly felt it was.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Well, maybe -- maybe it was. All I said was when I saw --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: We --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: -- the headings that scienter was required, I didn't think I'd find under it a discussion of hearing that may --

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Well --

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I am sorry, Mr. Justice Brennan. This was one of them, and in their -- I think there were others and I don't have them before me now.

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: My recollection was that after the admissions were made, an argument was had before the trial court, I prepared the findings of fact and the conclusions of law and they were presented to the Attorney General's office. They incorporated finding number 6, they were approved and in -- in accordance with the original complaint which initiated the action.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Well, your arguments would not -- before the trial court, your arguments or the other side's arguments?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: There were. We presented a written brief and the state's Attorney General --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Are they in the record?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: They are not, You Honor.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Are they available?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: I believe they are.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Would each of you supply us with -- with your briefs, please, a copy of your brief?

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes, we will, Your Honor.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: We have your brief in the record, but I do not think --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Not the trial court.

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Not the trial court.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: No, no because that means --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: Is it their briefs in the trial court?

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Yes, but even beyond. In the Supreme Court, we have the appellant brief, but we do not have the state's brief, Mr. Chief Justice.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: The state wasn't a company --

turns.name: HUGO L. BLACK
turns.text: But the State have a --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: The State of the appellant in the Supreme Court of -- of --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: And we do not --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Mr. Justice Frankfurter --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: We do not have that. We do have the -- the present appellant's brief.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Yes. The --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Would you supply that too, please, Mr. Fuller?

turns.name: HERBERT H. FULLER
turns.text: I will, Mr. Chief Justice.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Thank you very much.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: The state's brief in the State Supreme Court only argued the state constitutional issues, did not argue the federal constitutional issues which had been raised. They were the appellants --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Yes, isn't it?

turns.name: WILLIAM J. BRENNAN, JR.
turns.text: What you had advanced (Inaudible)?

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: We'll do so, Mr. Justice Brennan. May I just mention, our brief in opposition to the motion to dismiss, I think that we discussed that. A motion to dismiss was made upon this precise ground.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.name: SOLIE M. RINGOLD
turns.text: Thank you.
